name: timds
description: React component library with a built-in design system (shadcn/ui new-york + Tailwind v4). Consumed as a compiled package: import components from `timds` and styles from `timds/styles.css`.

setup.darkMode: Add the `dark` class to an ancestor (e.g.: <html class="dark">). Opt-in.
setup.font: The DS uses the Inter family (`--font-sans`), but does NOT load the font. Load Inter in your app (e.g.: a Google Fonts <link> or @font-face in index.html); without it, it falls back to system-ui.
setup.charts: To use Chart/ChartContainer, install `recharts` (>=3) in the app: `npm i recharts`. It is a peerDependency (a single instance shared between the app and the DS); the chart primitives (AreaChart, Pie, …) come from `recharts`, and ChartContainer/ChartTooltip… from `timds`.
rules: Import ALL components from `timds` — never from subpaths.
Import `timds/styles.css` from a CSS file, right AFTER `@import "tailwindcss"` (not via JS) — see `setup.importStyles`.
Load the Inter font in your app (the DS does not load it) — see `setup.font`.

To also use token utilities (bg-primary, etc.) in your own markup, add the full `@theme` bridge from the `setup.tailwindBridge` field (it already includes the Tailwind + `timds/styles.css` imports; requires Tailwind v4 in the app).
Use only semantic color utilities (bg-primary, text-muted-foreground, etc.); never raw colors (bg-blue-500).
Vivid colors have a fill token (bg-success) and a text token (text-success-text). For text/icons use the `-text` one.
Icons via lucide-react. Icon-only buttons need an aria-label.

searchGuidance.message: For tags / chips / multi-value selection, use **Select** in multi-edit mode (`multiple`). timds has no dedicated Tag/Chip input — the multiple Select renders the chosen values as removable chips (`SelectChipsValue`). Get details with `get_component` → "Select".

components.name: Alert
components.description: A contextual feedback banner that surfaces a short message inline, in place on the page.
components.importStatement: import { Alert, AlertDescription, AlertTitle } from 'timds'

props.name: variant
props.description: Neutral, destructive (error) or one of the soft semantic styles (success, warning, info, accent).

components.example:
import { Alert, AlertTitle, AlertDescription } from 'timds'
import { CheckCircle2 } from 'lucide-react'

<Alert variant="success">
  <CheckCircle2 />
  <AlertTitle>Changes saved</AlertTitle>
  <AlertDescription>Your settings were updated successfully.</AlertDescription>
</Alert>

components.name: AlertDialog
components.description: A modal confirmation that interrupts the user and demands an explicit choice before a destructive or irreversible action.
components.importStatement: import { AlertDialog, AlertDialogAction, AlertDialogCancel, AlertDialogContent, AlertDialogDescription, AlertDialogFooter, AlertDialogHeader, AlertDialogMedia, AlertDialogOverlay, AlertDialogPortal, AlertDialogTitle, AlertDialogTrigger } from 'timds'
components.example:
import {
  AlertDialog,
  AlertDialogTrigger,
  AlertDialogContent,
  AlertDialogHeader,
  AlertDialogTitle,
  AlertDialogDescription,
  AlertDialogFooter,
  AlertDialogCancel,
  AlertDialogAction,
  Button,
} from 'timds'

<AlertDialog>
  <AlertDialogTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="destructive">Delete project</Button>
  </AlertDialogTrigger>
  <AlertDialogContent>
    <AlertDialogHeader>
      <AlertDialogTitle>Delete this project?</AlertDialogTitle>
      <AlertDialogDescription>This cannot be undone.</AlertDialogDescription>
    </AlertDialogHeader>
    <AlertDialogFooter>
      <AlertDialogCancel>Keep project</AlertDialogCancel>
      <AlertDialogAction variant="destructive" onClick={handleDelete}>
        Delete
      </AlertDialogAction>
    </AlertDialogFooter>
  </AlertDialogContent>
</AlertDialog>

components.name: Avatar
components.description: Represents a user or entity with an image, falling back to initials when no image is available.
components.importStatement: import { Avatar, AvatarBadge, AvatarFallback, AvatarGroup, AvatarGroupCount, AvatarImage } from 'timds'

props.name: size
props.description: Avatar diameter; propagated to badge and group count.

components.example:
import { Avatar, AvatarImage, AvatarFallback, AvatarBadge, AvatarGroup, AvatarGroupCount } from 'timds'

// Image with initials fallback
<Avatar>
  <AvatarImage src="/users/ada.png" alt="Ada Lovelace" />
  <AvatarFallback>AL</AvatarFallback>
</Avatar>

// With a status dot
<Avatar size="lg">
  <AvatarImage src="/users/ada.png" alt="Ada Lovelace" />
  <AvatarFallback>AL</AvatarFallback>
  <AvatarBadge className="bg-success" />
</Avatar>

// Stacked group
<AvatarGroup>
  <Avatar><AvatarFallback>AL</AvatarFallback></Avatar>
  <Avatar><AvatarFallback>CB</AvatarFallback></Avatar>
  <AvatarGroupCount>+3</AvatarGroupCount>
</AvatarGroup>

components.name: Badge
components.description: A compact pill that labels, counts, or surfaces the status of an item.
components.importStatement: import { Badge, badgeVariants } from 'timds'

props.name: size
props.description: Padding and font size of the badge.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Render the styling onto the child element (e.g. an

components.example: import { Badge } from 'timds' New Completed Pending // As a link (e.g. a filter chip) Filters

components.name: Button
components.description: Triggers an action or event — the primary way users commit to something in the UI.
components.importStatement: import { Button, buttonVariants, ButtonProps } from 'timds'

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual style / emphasis of the button.

props.name: icon
props.description: Icon node shown alongside the content.

props.name: loading
props.description: Show a spinner in place of the icon and disable the button.

props.name: block
props.description: Stretch the button to fill its container width.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Merge props onto the child element instead of rendering a

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables interaction and dims the button.

components.example:
import { Button } from 'timds'

<Button onClick={handleSave}>Save</Button>
<Button variant="secondary" onClick={handleCancel}>Cancel</Button>
<Button icon={<Rocket />} loading>Launching</Button>
<Button href="https://example.com">Open link</Button>

components.name: ButtonGroup
components.description: A layout wrapper that joins buttons (and related controls) into a single continuous segment, collapsing the inner borders and corners between them.
components.importStatement: import { ButtonGroup, ButtonGroupSeparator, ButtonGroupText, buttonGroupVariants } from 'timds'

props.name: orientation
props.description: Direction the buttons are stacked in.

components.example:
import { Button, ButtonGroup, ButtonGroupSeparator, ButtonGroupText } from 'timds'

// Joined actions
<ButtonGroup>
  <Button variant="outline">Archive</Button>
  <Button variant="outline">Report</Button>
  <Button variant="outline">Snooze</Button>
</ButtonGroup>

// Stepper with a static value
<ButtonGroup>
  <Button variant="outline" size="icon" aria-label="Decrease"><Minus /></Button>
  <ButtonGroupText>12</ButtonGroupText>
  <Button variant="outline" size="icon" aria-label="Increase"><Plus /></Button>
</ButtonGroup>

// Vertical stack
<ButtonGroup orientation="vertical">
  <Button variant="outline">Top</Button>
  <Button variant="outline">Bottom</Button>
</ButtonGroup>

components.name: Calendar
components.description: An inline month grid for selecting a single date, a range, or multiple days.
components.importStatement: import { Calendar, CalendarDayButton } from 'timds'

props.name: captionLayout
props.description: Header as a static label or month/year dropdowns.

props.name: numberOfMonths
props.description: How many months to render side by side.

props.name: showOutsideDays
props.description: Show leading/trailing days from adjacent months.

props.name: buttonVariant
props.description: Button variant used by the navigation arrows.

components.name: Card
components.description: A surface container that groups related content and actions into a single panel.
components.importStatement: import { Card, CardAction, CardContent, CardDescription, CardFooter, CardHeader, CardTitle } from 'timds'
components.example:
import { Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardAction, CardContent, CardFooter, Button, Badge, } from 'timds' Total revenue Last 30 days +9.8%

$125,830

components.name: Chart
components.description: A thin, themeable wrapper around [Recharts](https://recharts.org) that drives series colors, tooltips and legends from one design-system-aware config.
components.importStatement: import { ChartContainer, ChartLegend, ChartLegendContent, ChartStyle, ChartTooltip, ChartTooltipContent, ChartConfig } from 'timds'
components.example:
import {
  type ChartConfig,
  ChartContainer,
  ChartTooltip,
  ChartTooltipContent,
} from 'timds'
import { Area, AreaChart, CartesianGrid, XAxis } from 'recharts'

const config = {
  desktop: { label: 'Desktop', color: 'var(--chart-1)' },
} satisfies ChartConfig

<ChartContainer config={config} className="h-[240px] w-full">
  <AreaChart accessibilityLayer data={data}>
    <CartesianGrid vertical={false} />
    <XAxis dataKey="month" tickLine={false} axisLine={false} />
    <ChartTooltip cursor={false} content={<ChartTooltipContent />} />
    {/* the `desktop` color becomes the `--color-desktop` CSS variable */}
    <Area dataKey="desktop" fill="var(--color-desktop)" stroke="var(--color-desktop)" />
  </AreaChart>
</ChartContainer>

components.name: Checkbox
components.description: A boolean control for selecting an option, typically as part of a form submitted later.
components.importStatement: import { Checkbox, CheckboxGroup, CheckboxGroupItem, checkboxVariants, CheckboxGroupItemProps, CheckboxGroupProps, CheckboxProps } from 'timds'

props.name: variant
props.description: Chromatic scheme of the box when checked.

components.example:
import { Checkbox, CheckboxGroup, CheckboxGroupItem } from 'timds'

// Single checkbox paired with a label
<label htmlFor="terms" className="flex items-center gap-2">
  <Checkbox id="terms" />
  Accept the terms
</label>

// Controlled
<Checkbox checked={checked} onCheckedChange={(v) => setChecked(v === true)} />

// Indeterminate
<Checkbox checked="indeterminate" />

// Group of related values
<CheckboxGroup defaultValue={['react']} onValueChange={setValues}>
  <CheckboxGroupItem value="react" />
  <CheckboxGroupItem value="vue" />
</CheckboxGroup>

components.name: Context Menu
components.description: A menu of actions opened by right-clicking (or long-pressing) a region — the actions appear at the pointer and dismiss once the user picks one or moves away.
components.importStatement: import { ContextMenu, ContextMenuCheckboxItem, ContextMenuContent, ContextMenuGroup, ContextMenuItem, ContextMenuLabel, ContextMenuPortal, ContextMenuRadioGroup, ContextMenuRadioItem, ContextMenuSeparator, ContextMenuShortcut, ContextMenuSub, ContextMenuSubContent, ContextMenuSubTrigger, ContextMenuTrigger } from 'timds'
components.example:
import {
  ContextMenu,
  ContextMenuTrigger,
  ContextMenuContent,
  ContextMenuLabel,
  ContextMenuSeparator,
  ContextMenuItem,
  ContextMenuShortcut,
} from 'timds'

<ContextMenu>
  <ContextMenuTrigger className="flex h-32 w-72 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-dashed text-sm">
    Right click here
  </ContextMenuTrigger>
  <ContextMenuContent className="w-52">
    <ContextMenuLabel>Actions</ContextMenuLabel>
    <ContextMenuSeparator />
    <ContextMenuItem onSelect={handleReload}>
      Reload
      <ContextMenuShortcut>⌘R</ContextMenuShortcut>
    </ContextMenuItem>
    <ContextMenuItem variant="destructive" onSelect={handleDelete}>
      Delete
    </ContextMenuItem>
  </ContextMenuContent>
</ContextMenu>

components.name: DatePicker
components.description: A trigger that shows the selected date and opens a calendar popover to pick a single day.
components.importStatement: import { DatePicker, DatePickerProps } from 'timds'

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Text shown on the trigger while no date is selected.

props.name: formatStr
props.description: date-fns format string for the trigger label.

props.name: align
props.description: Popover alignment relative to the trigger.

components.name: Dialog
components.description: A modal overlay that focuses the user on a single task — a form, details, or a confirmation — before returning them to the page.
components.importStatement: import { Dialog, DialogClose, DialogContent, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, DialogHeader, DialogOverlay, DialogPortal, DialogTitle, DialogTrigger } from 'timds'
components.example:
import {
  Dialog,
  DialogTrigger,
  DialogContent,
  DialogHeader,
  DialogTitle,
  DialogDescription,
  DialogFooter,
  DialogClose,
  Button,
} from 'timds'

<Dialog>
  <DialogTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="outline">Edit profile</Button>
  </DialogTrigger>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Edit profile</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>Update your details.</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
    <DialogFooter>
      <DialogClose asChild>
        <Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button>
      </DialogClose>
      <DialogClose asChild>
        <Button onClick={handleSave}>Save changes</Button>
      </DialogClose>
    </DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

components.name: Drawer
components.description: A panel that slides in from an edge of the viewport to hold secondary content — filters, details, navigation, or a short form — without taking over the whole screen.
components.importStatement: import { Drawer, DrawerClose, DrawerContent, DrawerDescription, DrawerFooter, DrawerHeader, DrawerOverlay, DrawerPortal, DrawerTitle, DrawerTrigger } from 'timds'
components.example:
import {
  Drawer,
  DrawerTrigger,
  DrawerContent,
  DrawerHeader,
  DrawerTitle,
  DrawerDescription,
  DrawerFooter,
  DrawerClose,
  Button,
} from 'timds'

<Drawer direction="right">
  <DrawerTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="outline">Open panel</Button>
  </DrawerTrigger>
  <DrawerContent>
    <DrawerHeader>
      <DrawerTitle>Filters</DrawerTitle>
      <DrawerDescription>Refine the results shown in the list.</DrawerDescription>
    </DrawerHeader>
    <DrawerFooter>
      <Button onClick={handleApply}>Apply</Button>
      <DrawerClose asChild>
        <Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button>
      </DrawerClose>
    </DrawerFooter>
  </DrawerContent>
</Drawer>

components.name: Dropdown Menu
components.description: A menu of actions anchored to a trigger — opened on demand, dismissed once the user picks an action or moves away.
components.importStatement: import { DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuCheckboxItem, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuGroup, DropdownMenuItem, DropdownMenuLabel, DropdownMenuPortal, DropdownMenuRadioGroup, DropdownMenuRadioItem, DropdownMenuSeparator, DropdownMenuShortcut, DropdownMenuSub, DropdownMenuSubContent, DropdownMenuSubTrigger, DropdownMenuTrigger } from 'timds'
components.example:
import {
  DropdownMenu,
  DropdownMenuTrigger,
  DropdownMenuContent,
  DropdownMenuLabel,
  DropdownMenuSeparator,
  DropdownMenuItem,
  DropdownMenuShortcut,
  Button,
} from 'timds'

<DropdownMenu>
  <DropdownMenuTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="outline">Open menu</Button>
  </DropdownMenuTrigger>
  <DropdownMenuContent className="w-48">
    <DropdownMenuLabel>My account</DropdownMenuLabel>
    <DropdownMenuSeparator />
    <DropdownMenuItem onSelect={handleProfile}>
      Profile
      <DropdownMenuShortcut>⇧⌘P</DropdownMenuShortcut>
    </DropdownMenuItem>
    <DropdownMenuItem variant="destructive" onSelect={handleSignOut}>
      Sign out
    </DropdownMenuItem>
  </DropdownMenuContent>
</DropdownMenu>

components.name: Input
components.description: A single-line text field — the primary way users enter free-form text into a form.
components.importStatement: import { Input } from 'timds'

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Placeholder shown while the field is empty.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the field: not focusable, ignores input.

components.name: InputGroup
components.description: A container that wraps a text control with addons — icons, text or buttons — sharing a single border, focus ring and error state.
components.importStatement: import { InputGroup, InputGroupAddon, InputGroupButton, InputGroupInput, InputGroupText, InputGroupTextarea } from 'timds'
components.example:
import { InputGroup, InputGroupAddon, InputGroupInput, InputGroupButton, InputGroupText } from 'timds'
import { Search } from 'lucide-react'

// Search field with a leading icon
<InputGroup>
  <InputGroupAddon>
    <Search />
  </InputGroupAddon>
  <InputGroupInput placeholder="Search…" aria-label="Search" />
</InputGroup>

// Field with a trailing action button
<InputGroup>
  <InputGroupInput aria-label="Coupon" />
  <InputGroupAddon align="inline-end">
    <InputGroupButton onClick={apply}>Apply</InputGroupButton>
  </InputGroupAddon>
</InputGroup>

components.name: InputOTP
components.description: A fixed-length code field that splits a one-time password or verification code into per-character slots.
components.importStatement: import { InputOTP, InputOTPGroup, InputOTPSeparator, InputOTPSlot } from 'timds'

props.name: maxLength
props.description: Total number of characters/slots the field accepts.

props.name: onChange
props.description: Fires with the full string on every change.

components.example:
import { InputOTP, InputOTPGroup, InputOTPSlot, InputOTPSeparator } from 'timds'
import { REGEXP_ONLY_DIGITS } from 'input-otp'

<InputOTP maxLength={6} pattern={REGEXP_ONLY_DIGITS} onChange={setCode}>
  <InputOTPGroup>
    <InputOTPSlot index={0} />
    <InputOTPSlot index={1} />
    <InputOTPSlot index={2} />
  </InputOTPGroup>
  <InputOTPSeparator />
  <InputOTPGroup>
    <InputOTPSlot index={3} />
    <InputOTPSlot index={4} />
    <InputOTPSlot index={5} />
  </InputOTPGroup>
</InputOTP>

components.name: Item
components.description: A flexible row primitive for lists, cards, and menus — a leading media slot, a central title/description, and trailing actions, composed from small named parts.
components.importStatement: import { Item, ItemActions, ItemContent, ItemDescription, ItemFooter, ItemGroup, ItemHeader, ItemMedia, ItemSeparator, ItemTitle, itemVariants } from 'timds'

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual style of the row surface.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Merge props onto the single child instead of rendering a

components.example: import { Avatar, AvatarFallback, AvatarImage, Button, Item, ItemActions, ItemContent, ItemDescription, ItemGroup, ItemMedia, ItemSeparator, ItemTitle, } from 'timds' // Basic row with a trailing action Basic Item A simple row with title and description. // The whole row as a link Visit our documentation Learn how to get started. // A grouped, separated list S shadcn shadcn@vercel.com {/* …more items */}

components.name: Pagination
components.description: Splits long content into pages and lets people move between them.
components.importStatement: import { getPaginationRange, Pagination, PaginationContent, PaginationEllipsis, PaginationItem, PaginationLink, PaginationNext, PaginationPrevious, PaginationShort, PaginationMode, PaginationRangeItem, PaginationRangeOptions, PaginationShortLabels, PaginationShortProps } from 'timds'

props.name: siblingCount
props.description: Sibling pages around the current one (full/numbers).

props.name: pageSizeOptions
props.description: Rows-per-page choices; renders the selector when provided.

props.name: onPageChange
props.description: Called with the chosen page (SPA mode).

props.name: onPageSizeChange
props.description: Called with the chosen rows-per-page value.

props.name: href
props.description: Builds each page href (real-links/SSR mode); wins over onPageChange.

components.name: Popover
components.description: A floating panel anchored to a trigger, for secondary content shown on demand.
components.importStatement: import { Popover, PopoverAnchor, PopoverContent, PopoverDescription, PopoverHeader, PopoverTitle, PopoverTrigger } from 'timds'

props.name: align
props.description: Alignment of the content relative to the trigger.

props.name: side
props.description: Preferred side of the trigger to render on (flips on collision).

props.name: sideOffset
props.description: Distance in pixels between the content and the trigger.

components.example:
import { Button, Input, Popover, PopoverContent, PopoverHeader, PopoverTitle, PopoverTrigger } from 'timds'

<Popover>
  <PopoverTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="outline">Open</Button>
  </PopoverTrigger>
  <PopoverContent align="start">
    <PopoverHeader>
      <PopoverTitle>Dimensions</PopoverTitle>
    </PopoverHeader>
    <Input aria-label="Width" defaultValue="100%" />
  </PopoverContent>
</Popover>

components.name: Progress
components.description: A linear bar that communicates how far along a determinate task is, or that work of unknown duration is underway.
components.importStatement: import { Progress, progressVariants } from 'timds'

props.name: variant
props.description: Chromatic scheme of the track and indicator.

props.name: value
props.description: Current progress (0–100). Omit it for the indeterminate state.

components.name: RadioGroup
components.description: A set of mutually exclusive options where exactly one can be selected at a time.
components.importStatement: import { RadioGroup, RadioGroupItem } from 'timds'

props.name: onValueChange
props.description: Fired with the newly selected value.

components.example:
import { RadioGroup, RadioGroupItem } from 'timds'

// Uncontrolled
<RadioGroup defaultValue="yearly" aria-label="Plan">
  <label htmlFor="monthly">
    <RadioGroupItem id="monthly" value="monthly" /> Monthly
  </label>
  <label htmlFor="yearly">
    <RadioGroupItem id="yearly" value="yearly" /> Yearly
  </label>
</RadioGroup>

// Controlled
<RadioGroup value={value} onValueChange={setValue} aria-label="Plan">
  {/* …items */}
</RadioGroup>

components.name: Select
components.description: An advanced Select / Combobox built on **Ariakit** with search, multi-select, custom rendering, async loading and list virtualization — not the native `<select>`.
components.importStatement: import { Select, SelectContent, SelectEmpty, SelectGroup, SelectItem, SelectList, SelectLoading, SelectRoot, SelectSearch, SelectTrigger, SelectValue, selectTriggerVariants, SelectMessages, SelectOption, SelectProps } from 'timds'

props.name: editable
props.description: Editable trigger (autocomplete in the field itself).

props.name: allowCustomValue
props.description: Editable: accepts text outside the list.

props.name: hasMore
props.description: There are more items for infinite scroll.

components.name: Separator
components.description: A thin visual divider that groups or splits content, horizontally or vertically.
components.importStatement: import { Separator } from 'timds'

props.name: decorative
props.description: When true (default) the separator is purely visual and hidden from assistive tech.

components.example:
import { Separator } from 'timds' // Horizontal divider (decorative) // Inline vertical divider, inside a container with height
Docs Source
// Semantic: exposed to assistive technologies

components.name: Sidebar
components.description: A composable, collapsible application sidebar — the persistent navigation frame for a page, with a desktop layout and a mobile off-canvas overlay built in.
components.importStatement: import { Sidebar, SidebarContent, SidebarFooter, SidebarGroup, SidebarGroupAction, SidebarGroupContent, SidebarGroupLabel, SidebarHeader, SidebarInput, SidebarInset, SidebarMenu, SidebarMenuAction, SidebarMenuBadge, SidebarMenuButton, SidebarMenuItem, SidebarMenuSkeleton, SidebarMenuSub, SidebarMenuSubButton, SidebarMenuSubItem, SidebarProvider, SidebarRail, SidebarSeparator, SidebarTrigger, sidebarMenuButtonVariants, useSidebar } from 'timds'

props.name: side
props.description: Which edge the sidebar is anchored to.

props.name: collapsible
props.description: How it collapses on desktop: slide

components.example:
import { Sidebar, SidebarContent, SidebarGroup, SidebarGroupContent, SidebarGroupLabel, SidebarInset, SidebarMenu, SidebarMenuButton, SidebarMenuItem, SidebarProvider, SidebarTrigger, } from 'timds' export function AppShell({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { return ( Platform Home
{children} ) }

components.name: Skeleton
components.description: A pulsing placeholder that reserves layout space while real content loads.
components.importStatement: import { Skeleton } from 'timds'

components.name: Slider
components.description: An input for picking a numeric value — or a range — by dragging a thumb along a track.
components.importStatement: import { sliderRangeVariants, Slider, sliderThumbVariants, SliderProps } from 'timds'

props.name: variant
props.description: Chromatic scheme of the filled range and thumb.

components.name: Spinner
components.description: An animated indicator that communicates an ongoing, indeterminate loading state.
components.importStatement: import { Spinner } from 'timds'

components.name: Switch
components.description: A boolean toggle that flips a single setting on or off, taking effect immediately.
components.importStatement: import { Switch, switchVariants, SwitchProps } from 'timds'

props.name: variant
props.description: Chromatic scheme of the track (on / off colors).

components.name: Table
components.description: Displays tabular data in rows and columns, built on native HTML table elements.
components.importStatement: import { Table, TableBody, TableCaption, TableCell, TableFooter, TableGroupRow, TableHead, TableHeader, TableRow } from 'timds'
components.example: import { Table, TableBody, TableCaption, TableCell, TableHead, TableHeader, TableRow, } from 'timds' Recent invoices. Invoice Amount {invoices.map((row) => ( {row.invoice} {row.amount} ))}

components.name: Tabs
components.description: Switches between sibling panels of content within the same context, one visible at a time.
components.importStatement: import { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger, tabsListVariants } from 'timds'

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: Value of the tab selected on mount (uncontrolled).

props.name: tabPlacement
props.description: Placement of the tab bar relative to the content.

components.example:
import { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from 'timds'

<Tabs defaultValue="home">
  <TabsList variant="pill">
    <TabsTrigger value="home">Home</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="products">Products</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="home">Home panel</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="products">Products panel</TabsContent>
</Tabs>

// Editable tabs
<TabsTrigger value="1" closable onClose={() => remove('1')}>
  Tab 1
</TabsTrigger>

components.name: Textarea
components.description: A multi-line text field for free-form input that can span several lines.
components.importStatement: import { Textarea } from 'timds'

props.name: rows
props.description: Initial visible number of text lines.

components.name: Toaster
components.description: Brief, non-blocking notifications anchored to a corner of the screen. Built on [sonner](https://sonner.emilkowal.ski/): mount `<Toaster />` once, then fire toasts from anywhere with the imperative `toast` function.
components.importStatement: import { Toaster, toast, ToasterProps } from 'timds'

props.name: position
props.description: Where the stack of toasts is anchored on screen.

props.name: richColors
props.description: Colorize toasts by type (success/error/warning/info) using the design tokens.

props.name: closeButton
props.description: Render an explicit close (×) button on every toast.

props.name: expand
props.description: Keep toasts expanded instead of stacking them collapsed.

props.name: duration
props.description: Default auto-dismiss time in ms (per toast can override).

props.name: visibleToasts
props.description: How many toasts are shown at once before older ones collapse.

props.name: theme
props.description: Sonner’s internal theme. Colors here come from tokens, so this rarely matters;

components.name: Toggle
components.description: A two-state button that stays pressed once activated — for binary, on/off controls like a formatting mark in a toolbar.
components.importStatement: import { Toggle, toggleVariants } from 'timds'

components.name: ToggleGroup
components.description: A set of **Toggle** buttons that share styling and coordinate selection — the building block for segmented controls and formatting toolbars.
components.importStatement: import { ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem } from 'timds'

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual style shared by every item.

props.name: size
props.description: Item height shared by every item.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables every item in the group.

components.example:
import { ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem } from 'timds'
import { AlignLeft, AlignCenter, AlignRight } from 'lucide-react'

// Single selection (radiogroup)
<ToggleGroup type="single" defaultValue="left" aria-label="Text alignment">
  <ToggleGroupItem value="left" aria-label="Align left">
    <AlignLeft />
  </ToggleGroupItem>
  <ToggleGroupItem value="center" aria-label="Align center">
    <AlignCenter />
  </ToggleGroupItem>
  <ToggleGroupItem value="right" aria-label="Align right">
    <AlignRight />
  </ToggleGroupItem>
</ToggleGroup>

components.name: Tooltip
components.description: A small floating label that explains an element on hover or keyboard focus.
components.importStatement: import { Tooltip, TooltipContent, TooltipProvider, TooltipTrigger } from 'timds'
components.example:
import { Button, Tooltip, TooltipContent, TooltipProvider, TooltipTrigger } from 'timds'

function SaveButton() {
  return (
    <TooltipProvider>
      <Tooltip>
        <TooltipTrigger asChild>
          <Button variant="outline" size="icon" aria-label="Save">
            <SaveIcon />
          </Button>
        </TooltipTrigger>
        <TooltipContent>Save changes</TooltipContent>
      </Tooltip>
    </TooltipProvider>
  )
}

examples.name: Dashboard
examples.description: A full analytics dashboard: a collapsible `Sidebar` + `SidebarInset` shell, a sticky header, KPI `Card`s with trend `Badge`s, an area and a donut `Chart`, and a recent-sales `Table`. The capstone composition.
examples.source:
import type { Meta, StoryObj } from '@storybook/react-vite' import { ArrowDownRight, ArrowUpRight, BarChart3, Bell, CreditCard, DollarSign, LayoutDashboard, type LucideIcon, Search, Settings, Users, } from 'lucide-react' import { Area, AreaChart, CartesianGrid, Pie, PieChart, XAxis } from 'recharts' import { expect, within } from 'storybook/test' import { Avatar, AvatarFallback, AvatarImage } from '@/components/ui/avatar' import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle, } from '@/components/ui/card' import { type ChartConfig, ChartContainer, ChartLegend, ChartLegendContent, ChartTooltip, ChartTooltipContent, } from '@/components/ui/chart' import { DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem, DropdownMenuLabel, DropdownMenuSeparator, DropdownMenuTrigger, } from '@/components/ui/dropdown-menu' import { InputGroup, InputGroupAddon, InputGroupInput } from '@/components/ui/input-group' import { Sidebar, SidebarContent, SidebarFooter, SidebarGroup, SidebarGroupContent, SidebarGroupLabel, SidebarHeader, SidebarInset, SidebarMenu, SidebarMenuBadge, SidebarMenuButton, SidebarMenuItem, SidebarProvider, SidebarRail, SidebarTrigger, } from '@/components/ui/sidebar' import { Table, TableBody, TableCell, TableHead, TableHeader, TableRow, } from '@/components/ui/table' /** * **Dashboard** — the most complete example: an application shell with a * collapsible `Sidebar`, a sticky header, metric cards with trend indicators, * two charts (`Chart`/Recharts) and a recent-sales table. It brings together * almost every design system family into a single coherent screen. */ const meta = { title: 'Examples/Dashboard', parameters: { layout: 'fullscreen', docs: { story: { inline: false, height: '720px' }, description: { component: 'A full analytics dashboard: a collapsible `Sidebar` + `SidebarInset` ' + 'shell, a sticky header, KPI `Card`s with trend `Badge`s, an area and a ' + 'donut `Chart`, and a recent-sales `Table`. The capstone composition.', }, }, }, } satisfies Meta export default meta type Story = StoryObj /* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */ /* Data */ /* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */ const revenueData = [ { month: 'Jan', receita: 4200, despesa: 2400 }, { month: 'Feb', receita: 5100, despesa: 2800 }, { month: 'Mar', receita: 4800, despesa: 3100 }, { month: 'Apr', receita: 6300, despesa: 3300 }, { month: 'May', receita: 7200, despesa: 3600 }, { month: 'Jun', receita: 8100, despesa: 3900 }, ] const revenueConfig = { receita: { label: 'Revenue', color: 'var(--chart-1)' }, despesa: { label: 'Expenses', color: 'var(--chart-2)' }, } satisfies ChartConfig const channelData = [ { channel: 'organico', visitas: 1240, fill: 'var(--color-organico)' }, { channel: 'social', visitas: 860, fill: 'var(--color-social)' }, { channel: 'pago', visitas: 540, fill: 'var(--color-pago)' }, { channel: 'direto', visitas: 420, fill: 'var(--color-direto)' }, ] const channelConfig = { visitas: { label: 'Visits' }, organico: { label: 'Organic', color: 'var(--chart-1)' }, social: { label: 'Social', color: 'var(--chart-2)' }, pago: { label: 'Paid', color: 'var(--chart-3)' }, direto: { label: 'Direct', color: 'var(--chart-4)' }, } satisfies ChartConfig const kpis: { label: string value: string delta: string up: boolean icon: LucideIcon }[] = [ { label: 'Revenue', value: '$45,231', delta: '+20.1%', up: true, icon: DollarSign }, { label: 'Subscribers', value: '2,350', delta: '+12.4%', up: true, icon: Users }, { label: 'Sales', value: '1,024', delta: '+8.2%', up: true, icon: CreditCard }, { label: 'Churn', value: '38', delta: '-3.1%', up: false, icon: ArrowDownRight, }, ] const sales = [ { name: 'Ana Souza', email: 'ana@timds.dev', amount: '$1,999', img: 47 }, { name: 'Bruno Lima', email: 'bruno@timds.dev', amount: '$39', img: 12 }, { name: 'Carla Dias', email: 'carla@timds.dev', amount: '$299', img: 5 }, { name: 'Diego Reis', email: 'diego@timds.dev', amount: '$99', img: 33 }, ] const navItems: { title: string; icon: LucideIcon; badge?: string }[] = [ { title: 'Dashboard', icon: LayoutDashboard }, { title: 'Analytics', icon: BarChart3 }, { title: 'Customers', icon: Users, badge: '24' }, { title: 'Payments', icon: CreditCard }, { title: 'Settings', icon: Settings }, ] /* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */ /* Subcomponents */ /* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */ function KpiCard({ label, value, delta, up, icon: Icon }: (typeof kpis)[number]) { return ( {label} {value} {up ? ( ) : ( )} {delta} ) } function DashboardApp() { return (
t
timds Analytics
Platform {navItems.map((item, i) => ( {item.title} {item.badge ? ( {item.badge} ) : null} ))} AS
Ana Souza ana@timds.dev
{/* Sticky header */}

Dashboard

My account Profile Billing Log out
{/* Content */}
{/* KPIs */}
{kpis.map((kpi) => ( ))}
{/* Charts */}
Revenue vs. Expenses Last 6 months } /> } /> Traffic by channel Source of visits } /> } className="flex-wrap gap-2" />
{/* Recent sales */} Recent sales You made 265 sales this month. Customer Amount {sales.map((sale) => (
{sale.name .split(' ') .map((n) => n[0]) .join('')}
{sale.name} {sale.email}
{sale.amount} ))}
) } export const Default: Story = { render: () => , play: async ({ canvasElement }) => { const canvas = within(canvasElement) await expect(canvas.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Dashboard' })).toBeInTheDocument() await expect(canvas.getByText('$45,231')).toBeInTheDocument() // The navigation marks the active item. await expect(canvas.getByRole('button', { name: 'Dashboard' })).toHaveAttribute( 'data-active', 'true', ) }, }

examples.name: Login
examples.description: A focused authentication card composed from `Card`, `InputGroup`, `Button`, `Checkbox` and `Separator`. Inputs are labelled, the icon-only nature of the addons is decorative, and every color comes from a semantic token.
examples.source:
import type { Meta, StoryObj } from '@storybook/react-vite' import { Lock, Mail } from 'lucide-react' import { expect, within } from 'storybook/test' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardFooter, CardHeader, CardTitle, } from '@/components/ui/card' import { Checkbox } from '@/components/ui/checkbox' import { InputGroup, InputGroupAddon, InputGroupInput } from '@/components/ui/input-group' import { Separator } from '@/components/ui/separator' /** * **Login** — the simplest example in the set. Shows how a single `Card` * organizes a short form: fields with an icon (`InputGroup`), primary and * secondary actions, a semantic divider and social providers. Everything is * backed by the design system tokens, without any loose color. */ const meta = { title: 'Examples/Login', parameters: { layout: 'fullscreen', docs: { description: { component: 'A focused authentication card composed from `Card`, `InputGroup`, ' + '`Button`, `Checkbox` and `Separator`. Inputs are labelled, the ' + 'icon-only nature of the addons is decorative, and every color comes ' + 'from a semantic token.', }, }, }, } satisfies Meta export default meta type Story = StoryObj export const Default: Story = { render: () => (
Access your account Sign in with your email and password to continue
Forgot?
or continue with

Don't have an account?{' '} Create one now

), play: async ({ canvasElement }) => { const canvas = within(canvasElement) // The title and the main CTA mount, and the email has an associated label. await expect(canvas.getByText('Access your account')).toBeInTheDocument() await expect(canvas.getByLabelText('Email')).toBeInTheDocument() await expect(canvas.getByRole('button', { name: 'Sign in' })).toBeInTheDocument() }, }

examples.name: Notifications
examples.description: A filterable activity feed: `Tabs` switch between all / unread / mentions, each row is an `Item` with media, content and actions, and an unread dot plus a count `Badge` track local state.

examples.name: Pricing
examples.description: A pricing section: three `Card` tiers, a recommended tier highlighted with a brand ring and a `Badge`, feature lists with success-colored check icons, and a monthly/yearly `Tabs` toggle that recomputes prices.
examples.source:
import type { Meta, StoryObj } from '@storybook/react-vite' import { Check, Sparkles } from 'lucide-react' import { useState } from 'react' import { expect, userEvent, within } from 'storybook/test' import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardFooter, CardHeader, CardTitle, } from '@/components/ui/card' import { Separator } from '@/components/ui/separator' import { ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem } from '@/components/ui/toggle-group' /** * **Pricing** — a marketing page. Shows how `Card` + `Badge` + `Button` * compose a plan comparison, with a visual highlight on the recommended plan * (border and ring in the brand color) and a period selector in `Tabs` that * recomputes the prices. */ const meta = { title: 'Examples/Pricing', parameters: { layout: 'fullscreen', docs: { description: { component: 'A pricing section: three `Card` tiers, a recommended tier highlighted ' + 'with a brand ring and a `Badge`, feature lists with success-colored ' + 'check icons, and a monthly/yearly `Tabs` toggle that recomputes prices.', }, }, }, } satisfies Meta export default meta type Story = StoryObj type Plan = { name: string description: string monthly: number features: string[] cta: string variant: 'outline' | 'default' featured?: boolean } const plans: Plan[] = [ { name: 'Starter', description: 'For personal projects and prototypes.', monthly: 0, features: ['1 project', '5k events/month', 'Basic dashboards', 'Email support'], cta: 'Start for free', variant: 'outline', }, { name: 'Pro', description: 'For growing teams.', monthly: 29, features: [ 'Unlimited projects', '1 million events/month', 'Advanced dashboards', 'Integrations and webhooks', 'Priority support', ], cta: 'Subscribe to Pro', variant: 'default', featured: true, }, { name: 'Enterprise', description: 'For organizations at scale.', monthly: 99, features: [ 'Everything in Pro', 'SSO and SAML', 'Custom limits', 'Dedicated SLA', 'Account manager', ], cta: 'Talk to sales', variant: 'outline', }, ] function PricingTable() { const [period, setPeriod] = useState<'monthly' | 'yearly'>('monthly') const yearly = period === 'yearly' return (
Simple pricing

Choose the plan that's right for you

Start for free and grow as you scale. No surprises, cancel anytime.

v && setPeriod(v as 'monthly' | 'yearly')} aria-label="Billing period" > Monthly Yearly -20%
{plans.map((plan) => { const price = yearly ? Math.round(plan.monthly * 12 * 0.8) : plan.monthly const suffix = plan.monthly === 0 ? '' : yearly ? '/year' : '/month' return ( {plan.featured ? ( Most popular ) : null} {plan.name} {plan.description}
R$ {price} {suffix ? ( {suffix} ) : null}
    {plan.features.map((feature) => (
  • {feature}
    • ))}
) })}
) } export const Default: Story = { render: () => , play: async ({ canvasElement }) => { const canvas = within(canvasElement) // Monthly, Pro costs R$ 29; switching to yearly applies the discount (12×0.8). await expect(canvas.getByText('R$ 29')).toBeInTheDocument() await userEvent.click(canvas.getByText('Yearly')) await expect(canvas.getByText('R$ 278')).toBeInTheDocument() }, }

examples.name: Profile
examples.description: A profile page: a gradient cover, an overlapping `Avatar`, identity metadata with `Badge`s, a stats row and a tabbed body whose activity feed is an `ItemGroup`.
examples.source:
import type { Meta, StoryObj } from '@storybook/react-vite' import { CalendarDays, GitCommitHorizontal, MapPin, MessageSquare, Settings, Star, UserPlus, } from 'lucide-react' import { expect, within } from 'storybook/test' import { Avatar, AvatarFallback, AvatarImage } from '@/components/ui/avatar' import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Card, CardContent } from '@/components/ui/card' import { Item, ItemContent, ItemDescription, ItemMedia, ItemTitle, } from '@/components/ui/item' import { Separator } from '@/components/ui/separator' import { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from '@/components/ui/tabs' /** * **Profile** — an identity page. Combines `Avatar`, `Badge`, `Card` and an * `ItemGroup` for the activity feed, organized by `Tabs`. Demonstrates how * a cover band and an overlapping header are assembled using only tokens and radius. */ const meta = { title: 'Examples/Profile', parameters: { layout: 'fullscreen', docs: { description: { component: 'A profile page: a gradient cover, an overlapping `Avatar`, identity ' + 'metadata with `Badge`s, a stats row and a tabbed body whose activity ' + 'feed is an `ItemGroup`.', }, }, }, } satisfies Meta export default meta type Story = StoryObj const stats = [ { label: 'Projects', value: '24' }, { label: 'Followers', value: '1.2k' }, { label: 'Following', value: '318' }, { label: 'Stars', value: '4.9' }, ] const activity = [ { icon: GitCommitHorizontal, title: 'Pushed 12 commits to timds/design-system', when: '2 hours ago', }, { icon: Star, title: 'Starred recharts/recharts', when: '5 hours ago', }, { icon: MessageSquare, title: 'Commented on issue #128 — Contrast tokens', when: 'yesterday', }, { icon: UserPlus, title: 'Started following Bruno Lima', when: '2 days ago', }, ] export const Default: Story = { render: () => (
{/* Cover */}
{/* Header overlapping the cover */}
AS

Ana Souza

Pro

Design Engineer · @anasouza

Building accessible and well-documented design systems. Passionate about tokens, typography and good defaults.

São Paulo, BR Joined Mar 2023
{/* Stats */}
{stats.map((stat) => (
{stat.value} {stat.label}
))}
{/* Tabbed body */} Activity About
{activity.map((entry) => ( {entry.title} {entry.when} ))}

Design engineer focused on reusable systems, accessibility and developer experience. Maintainer of timds.

), play: async ({ canvasElement }) => { const canvas = within(canvasElement) await expect(canvas.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Ana Souza' })).toBeInTheDocument() // The activity tab is the default and lists the feed. await expect(canvas.getByText(/Pushed 12 commits/)).toBeInTheDocument() }, }

examples.name: Settings
examples.description: A settings screen split by `Tabs` (account, notifications, security). Each section is a `Card`; preferences are labelled `Switch` rows; the security tab ends with a destructive "danger zone".
examples.source:
import type { Meta, StoryObj } from '@storybook/react-vite' import { Bell, Shield, User } from 'lucide-react' import type { ReactNode } from 'react' import { expect, userEvent, within } from 'storybook/test' import { Avatar, AvatarFallback, AvatarImage } from '@/components/ui/avatar' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardFooter, CardHeader, CardTitle, } from '@/components/ui/card' import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input' import { Separator } from '@/components/ui/separator' import { Switch } from '@/components/ui/switch' import { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from '@/components/ui/tabs' import { Textarea } from '@/components/ui/textarea' /** * **Settings** — a dense settings screen organized by `Tabs`. It shows the * pairing of `Card` for sectioning, `Switch` with accessible labels for * preferences, `Input`/`Textarea` for fields and a "danger zone" using the * `destructive` token. */ const meta = { title: 'Examples/Settings', parameters: { layout: 'fullscreen', docs: { description: { component: 'A settings screen split by `Tabs` (account, notifications, security). ' + 'Each section is a `Card`; preferences are labelled `Switch` rows; the ' + 'security tab ends with a destructive "danger zone".', }, }, }, } satisfies Meta export default meta type Story = StoryObj // Preference row: title + description on the left, Switch on the right. function ToggleRow({ id, title, description, defaultChecked, }: { id: string title: string description: string defaultChecked?: boolean }) { return (

{description}

) } function Field({ id, label, children, }: { id: string label: string children: ReactNode }) { return (
{children}
) } export const Default: Story = { render: () => (

Settings

Manage your account, preferences and security.

Account Notifications Security {/* Account */} Profile This information is shown publicly.
AS

examples.name: Sign Up
examples.description: A registration form that grows the Login pattern: labelled fields, a reveal-password button inside an `InputGroup`, a live password-strength `Progress`, a data-driven `Select` and a terms `Checkbox`.
examples.source:
import type { Meta, StoryObj } from '@storybook/react-vite' import { Eye, EyeOff, UserPlus } from 'lucide-react' import { useState } from 'react' import { expect, userEvent, within } from 'storybook/test' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardFooter, CardHeader, CardTitle, } from '@/components/ui/card' import { Checkbox } from '@/components/ui/checkbox' import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input' import { InputGroup, InputGroupAddon, InputGroupButton, InputGroupInput, } from '@/components/ui/input-group' import { Progress } from '@/components/ui/progress' import { Select } from '@/components/ui/select' /** * **Sign Up** — a step beyond Login: more fields, a password-strength meter * (`Progress`) that reacts to input, reveal/hide password with * `InputGroupButton` and a data-driven `Select` for the user's role. */ const meta = { title: 'Examples/Sign Up', parameters: { layout: 'fullscreen', docs: { description: { component: 'A registration form that grows the Login pattern: labelled fields, a ' + 'reveal-password button inside an `InputGroup`, a live password-strength ' + '`Progress`, a data-driven `Select` and a terms `Checkbox`.', }, }, }, } satisfies Meta export default meta type Story = StoryObj const roles = [ { value: 'eng', label: 'Engineering' }, { value: 'design', label: 'Design' }, { value: 'product', label: 'Product' }, { value: 'marketing', label: 'Marketing' }, { value: 'other', label: 'Other' }, ] // Password strength: 0–4 → label + Progress color variant. function strengthOf(value: string) { let score = 0 if (value.length >= 8) score++ if (/[A-Z]/.test(value)) score++ if (/[0-9]/.test(value)) score++ if (/[^A-Za-z0-9]/.test(value)) score++ const levels = [ { label: 'Very weak', variant: 'destructive' as const }, { label: 'Weak', variant: 'destructive' as const }, { label: 'Fair', variant: 'warning' as const }, { label: 'Good', variant: 'info' as const }, { label: 'Strong', variant: 'success' as const }, ][score] return { score, ...levels } } function SignUpForm() { const [password, setPassword] = useState('') const [show, setShow] = useState(false) const strength = strengthOf(password) return (
Create your account It takes less than a minute
Role

examples.name: Team
examples.description: A data-management screen: a searchable, filterable, paginated `Table` with row selection, avatars, role and status `Badge`s and per-row `DropdownMenu` actions — wrapped in a `Card` toolbar.
